name: B. P. S.
description_short: BP Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit financial cooperative founded in 1937, serving BP employees, contractors, and eligible members with over $100 million in assets.
description_long:
BP Federal Credit Union (BPFCU) was established in 1937 as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative primarily serving BP employees and their contractors worldwide. Over the decades, it has grown to serve thousands of members with assets exceeding $100 million. The credit union operates three physical branches on BP's West Houston campus (Westlake One, Helios, and Memorial Drive locations) and maintains a digital presence through online and mobile banking platforms.

BPFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including deposit accounts (interest checking, savings, money market, and CDs), lending products (mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, personal loans, home equity, home improvement, recreational loans, and lines of credit), and ancillary services such as wire transfers, money orders, cashier's checks, bill pay, mobile check deposit, and access to over 55,000 surcharge-free ATMs worldwide through a service network.

What distinguishes BPFCU is its member-owned governance structure and focus on serving a specific employment community while remaining accessible to qualifying contractors and employees of affiliated companies. The credit union emphasizes competitive rates (auto loans as low as 5.90% APR, personal loans as low as 9.90% APR) and member-centric benefits like no-cost bill pay and extensive ATM access. Their CEO has publicly articulated the credit union difference: member control and benefit rather than shareholder profit orientation.

As a credit union, BPFCU operates under NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) insurance rather than FDIC protection, and membership eligibility is restricted to BP employees, contractors, and employees of partner organizations—not open to the general public. While the institution offers robust services and competitive rates, potential members must first qualify for membership, and the organization's smaller size compared to major banks may mean fewer branch locations and potentially different technology features.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Personal loans and lines of credit
Mobile and online banking with check deposit
AutoSMART vehicle research and financing tool
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.90% APR for members
Access to 55,000+ surcharge-free ATMs worldwide through credit union network
No-cost bill pay and mobile check deposit via phone/tablet
Personal loans available without collateral requirement, only signature and good credit needed
NCUA-insured member accounts with not-for-profit, member-controlled governance
Multiple account types including interest-bearing checking and money market accounts
Comprehensive loan portfolio including mortgages, home equity, and recreational vehicle loans
cons: Membership restricted to BP employees, contractors, and eligible partner company employees—not open to general public
Limited branch presence with only three physical locations, all on BP's West Houston campus
Smaller institution with less than $100 million in assets may offer fewer advanced digital features than larger banks
Personal loan APR floor of 9.90% is higher than some alternative lenders
Website does not publish current rates for mortgages, home equity, or home improvement loans
best_for: Current and former BP employees seeking competitive rates and member-focused service
Employees of BP-affiliated or partner companies who qualify for membership
Credit union members prioritizing access to nationwide ATM networks and surcharge-free withdrawals
Borrowers with good credit seeking unsecured personal loans without collateral requirements
diagnosis: BPFCU is ideal for BP employees and contractors, or employees of affiliated companies, who value competitive rates, member-owned governance, and broad ATM access. The primary caveat is restricted membership eligibility—you must qualify as a BP-related employee or contractor to join, making this inaccessible to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: B. P. S. — NCUA-insured credit union in Beeville, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
